---
title: "Andrés Martínez Glez"
subtitle: "Economist · AIReF · Madrid"
image: profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 12em
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/AndresMtnezGlez
    - icon: file-earmark-text
      text: RPubs
      href: https://rpubs.com/andresmtnezglez
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/andr%C3%A9s-mart%C3%ADnez-8a034bba/
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:andres.mtnez.glez[at]gmail[.com]
---

I am an economist at the **Autoridad Independiente de Responsabilidad Fiscal (AIReF)**, Spain's independent fiscal institution, based in Madrid.

I work on applied fiscal policy and macroeconomic analysis, at the intersection of econometrics, EU fiscal governance, and long-run growth modelling. This page collects a set of technical notes and small projects in R that I wrote during my master's and the years after; mostly working through methods in time series, Bayesian computation, and statistical learning.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Views expressed on this site are my own and do not represent those of AIReF.
:::

## Research interests

- EU fiscal governance and the reformed fiscal rules
- Macroeconometrics: VAR and structural VAR models, time-series forecasting
- Long-term growth and potential output modelling
- Labour market and social security microdata analysis
